{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/pulls/comments/1807437150","pull_request_review_id":2379654479,"id":1807437150,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAA3dP85ru01e","diff_hunk":"@@ -541,8 +561,27 @@ def as_array(obj, shape=None):
 
 
     def as_ctypes(obj):
-        """Create and return a ctypes object from a numpy array.  Actually
-        anything that exposes the __array_interface__ is accepted."""
+        """
+        Create and return a ctypes object from a numpy array.  Actually
+        anything that exposes the __array_interface__ is accepted.
+
+        Examples
+        --------
+        Create ctypes object from inferred int ``np.array``:
+
+        >>> inferred_int_array = np.array([1, 2, 3])
+        >>> c_int_array = np.ctypeslib.as_ctypes(inferred_int_array)
+        >>> c_int_array
+        <c_long_Array_3 at 0x1071fbed0>
+
+        Create ctypes object from explicit 8 bit unsigned int ``np.array`` :
+
+        >>> exp_int_array = np.array([1, 2, 3], dtype=np.uint8)
+        >>> c_int_array = np.ctypeslib.as_ctypes(exp_int_array)
+        >>> c_int_array
+        <c_ubyte_Array_3 at 0x10755a950>","path":"numpy/ctypeslib.py","commit_id":"ce75474397238958c28f70b1a8a8eb3af3ec229d","original_commit_id":"c83ee2e232def58c040260a17e53c1d271a73bf9","user":{"login":"m-clare","id":6874646,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY4NzQ2NDY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6874646?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare","html_url":"https://github.com/m-clare","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I added a type check and printing the array contents itself. Mailing list should usually be notified."}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-23T10:49:04Z","updated_at":"2024-10-30T05:35:23Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

Some C-API functions have a limit in the number of arguments that can handle, given by the macro  `NPY_MAXARGS`. But that limit is not described in the documentation of the function `NpyIter_MultiNew`. Other functions related with iterators might be affected, but you don't know it by reading the documentation.


### Idea or request for content:

Those functions that have a limit in its arguments should have that limit documented.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27625/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27625/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2445902933","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27625#issuecomment-2445902933","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27625","id":2445902933,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86RyYRV","user":{"login":"m-clare","id":6874646,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY4NzQ2NDY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6874646?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare","html_url":"https://github.com/m-clare","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-clare/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-30T05:35:21Z","updated_at":"2024-10-30T05:35:21Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'd like to contribute to this if possible. I took a look at which C-API functions called `NpyIter_MultiNew` with a variable number of arguments (there are a few that call with `nop=2` or `nop=3` and turned up the following functions:

- [test_nditer_too_large](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/main/numpy/_core/src/multiarray/_multiarray_tests.c.src#L1123)
- [PyArray_MapIterNew](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/main/numpy/_core/src/multiarray/mapping.c#L2749)
- [arr_ravel_multi_index](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/main/numpy/_core/src/multiarray/compiled_base.c#L1091)

I think the first one is just for a test, but would the other two require documentation?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2445902933/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
